blocks.text: looked at Julian. He had noticed the man the moment he arrived â€” standing in the doorway, not family, not a guest. Watching.
had stayed back. He watched from the edge and said nothing until he had something to say.
had waited for the right moment to step in.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: From our porch. Two voices. Controlled. Four minutes, approximately. One voice went upstairs and didn't come back down.

blocks.speaker: Inspector Davenport
blocks.content: What do you do, Mr. Crane?

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: Behavioral analysis. Independent contractor. Fraud, corporate litigation mostly.

blocks.speaker: Inspector Davenport
blocks.content: I'll need a full statement from you. From both of you.

blocks.speaker: Inspector Davenport
blocks.content: I'll also need you to stay available. You may have been the only people who weren't inside the house.
He said it like it was procedural. It wasn't procedural. He was already thinking the same thing I was.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: We'll cooperate with everything you need.

options.label: He's sharper than he looks. He's already running the same math.
By the book. Experienced. He'll follow procedure before instinct.
He's open. He'll listen to someone who has more.
